fields.name: LPMS
fields.description: Low-power mode selection These bits select the low-power mode entered when the CPU enters the SleepDeep mode. 10x: Standby mode others reserved

fields.name: R2RSB1
fields.description: SRAM2 retention in Standby mode This bit is used to keep the SRAM2 content in Standby retention mode.

fields.name: ULPMEN
fields.description: BOR0 ultra-low-power mode. This bit is used to reduce the consumption by configuring the BOR0 in discontinuous mode for Stop 1 and Standby modes. Discontinuous mode is only available when BOR levels 1 to 4 and PVD are disabled. Note: This bit must be set to reach the lowest power consumption in the low-power modes. Note: This bit must not be set together with autonomous peripherals using HSI16 as kernel clock. Note: When BOR level 1 to 4 or PVD is enabled continuous mode applies independent from ULPMEN.

fields.name: RADIORSB
fields.description: 2.4 GHz RADIO SRAMs (RXTXRAM and Sequence RAM) and Sleep clock retention in Standby mode. This bit is used to keep the 2.4 GHz RADIO SRAMs content in Standby retention mode and the 2.4 GHz RADIO sleep timer counter operational.

fields.name: R1RSB1
fields.description: SRAM1 retention in Standby mode This bit is used to keep the SRAM1 content in Standby retention mode.

fields.name: SRAM1PDS1
fields.description: SRAM1 power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1) Note: The SRAM1 retention in Standby mode is controlled by R1RSB1 bit in PWR_CR1.

fields.name: SRAM2PDS1
fields.description: SRAM2 power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1) Note: The SRAM2 retention in Standby mode is controlled by R2RSB1 bit in PWR_CR1.

fields.name: ICRAMPDS
fields.description: ICACHE SRAM power-down in Stop modes (Stop 0, 1)

fields.name: FLASHFWU
fields.description: Flash memory fast wakeup from Stop modes (Stop 0, 1) This bit is used to obtain the best trade-off between low-power consumption and wakeup time when exiting the Stop 0 or Stop 1 modes. When this bit is set, the Flash memory remains in normal mode in Stop 0 and Stop 1 modes, which offers a faster startup time with higher consumption.

fields.name: REGSEL
fields.description: Regulator selection

fields.name: FSTEN
fields.description: Fast soft start

fields.name: VOSRDY
fields.description: Ready bit for VsubCORE/sub voltage scaling output selection Set and cleared by hardware. When decreasing the voltage scaling range, VOSRDY must be one before increasing the SYSCLK frequency.

fields.name: VOS
fields.description: Voltage scaling range selection Set a and cleared by software. Cleared by hardware when entering Stop 1 mode. Access can be secured by RCC SYSCLKSEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV.

registers.name: SVMCR
registers.description: PWR supply voltage monitoring control register

fields.name: PVDE
fields.description: Programmable voltage detector enable

fields.name: PVDLS
fields.description: Programmable voltage detector level selection These bits select the voltage threshold detected by the programmable voltage detector:

fields.name: WUPEN1
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pin WKUP1 enable Access can be secured by PWR WUP1SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV.

fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pin WKUP2 enable Access can be secured by PWR WUP2SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pin WKUP8 enable Access can be secured by PWR WUP8SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. fields.name: WUPP1
fields.description: Wakeup pin WKUP1 polarity. This bit must be configured when WUPEN1 = 0. Access can be secured by PWR WUP1SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV.

fields.name: WUSEL1
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pin WKUP1 selection This field must be configured when WUPEN1 = 0. Access can be secured by PWR WUP1SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV.

When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV.

This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV.

fields.name: DBP
fields.description: Disable Backup domain write protection In reset state, all registers and SRAM in Backup domain are protected against parasitic write access. This bit must be set to enable the write access to these registers.

fields.name: WUP1SEC
fields.description: WUP1 secure protection

fields.name: WUP2SEC
fields.description: WUP2 secure protection

fields.name: WUP3SEC
fields.description: WUP3 secure protection

fields.name: WUP4SEC
fields.description: WUP4 secure protection

fields.name: WUP5SEC
fields.description: WUP5 secure protection

fields.name: WUP6SEC
fields.description: WUP6 secure protection

fields.name: WUP7SEC
fields.description: WUP7 secure protection

fields.name: WUP8SEC
fields.description: WUP8 secure protection

fields.name: LPMSEC
fields.description: Low-power modes secure protection

fields.name: VDMSEC
fields.description: Voltage detection secure protection

fields.name: VBSEC
fields.description: Backup domain secure protection

fields.name: SPRIV
fields.description: PWR secure functions privilege configuration This bit is set and reset by software. It can be written only by a secure privileged access.

fields.name: NSPRIV
fields.description: PWR non-secure functions privilege configuration This bit is set and reset by software. It can be written only by privileged access, secure or non-secure.

fields.name: CSSF
fields.description: Clear Stop and Standby flags Access can be secured by PWR LPMSEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV. Writing 1 to this bit clears the STOPF and SBF flags.

fields.name: STOPF
fields.description: Stop flag This bit is set by hardware when the device enters a Stop or Standby mode at the same time as the sysclk has been set by hardware to select HSI16. It's cleared by software by writing 1 to the CSSF bit and by hardware when SBF is set.

fields.name: SBF
fields.description: Standby flag This bit is set by hardware when the device enters the Standby mode and the CPU restart from its reset vector. It's cleared by writing 1 to the CSSF bit, or by a power-on reset. It is not cleared by the system reset.

registers.name: SVMSR
registers.description: PWR supply voltage monitoring status register

fields.name: REGS
fields.description: Regulator selection

fields.name: PVDO
fields.description: Programmable voltage detector output

fields.name: ACTVOSRDY
fields.description: Voltage level ready for currently used VOS

fields.name: ACTVOS
fields.description: VOS currently applied to VsubCORE/sub This field provides the last VOS value.

fields.name: WUF1
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pending flag 1 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP1 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF1 bit of PWR_WUSCR or by hardware when WUPEN1 = 0.

fields.name: WUF2
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pending flag 2 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP2 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF2 bit of PWR_WUSCR or by hardware when WUPEN2 = 0.

fields.name: WUF3
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pending flag 3 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP3 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF3 bit of PWR_WUSCR or by hardware when WUPEN3 = 0.

fields.name: WUF4
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pending flag 4 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP4 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF4 bit of PWR_WUSCR or by hardware when WUPEN4 = 0.

fields.name: WUF5
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pending flag 5 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP5 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF5 bit of PWR_WUSCR or by hardware when WUPEN5 = 0.

fields.name: WUF6
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pending flag 6 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP6 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF6 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL6 different 11, or by hardware when WUPEN6 = 0. When WUSEL6 = 11, this bit is cleared by hardware when all associated internal wakeup source are cleared. When WUSEL6 = 11, no WKUP interrupt is generated

fields.name: WUF7
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pending flag 7 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP7 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF7 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL7 different 11, or by hardware when WUPEN7 = 0. When WUSEL7 = 11, this bit is cleared by hardware when all associated internal wakeup source are cleared. When WUSEL7 = 11, no WKUP interrupt is generated.

fields.name: WUF8
fields.description: Wakeup and interrupt pending flag 8 This bit is set when a wakeup event is detected on WKUP8 pin. This bit is cleared by writing 1 in the CWUF8 bit of PWR_WUSCR when WUSEL8 different 11, or by hardware when WUPEN8 = 0. When WUSEL8 = 11, this bit is cleared by hardware when all associated internal wakeup source are cleared. When WUSEL8 = 11, no WKUP interrupt is generated

fields.name: CWUF1
fields.description: Clear wakeup flag 1 Access can be secured by PWR WUP1SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV. Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF1 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF2
fields.description: Clear wakeup flag 2 Access can be secured by PWR WUP2SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV. Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF2 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF3
fields.description: Clear wakeup flag 3 Access can be secured by PWR WUP3SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV. Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF3 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF4
fields.description: Clear wakeup flag 4 Access can be secured by PWR WUP4SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV. Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF4 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF5
fields.description: Clear wakeup flag 5 Access can be secured by PWR WUP5SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV. Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF5 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF6
fields.description: Clear wakeup flag 6 Access can be secured by PWR WUP6SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV. Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF6 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF7
fields.description: Clear wakeup flag 7 Access can be secured by PWR WUP7SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV. Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF7 flag in PWR_WUSR.

fields.name: CWUF8
fields.description: Clear wakeup flag 8 Access can be secured by PWR WUP8SEC. When secure, a non-secure read/write access is RAZ/WI. It does not generate an illegal access interrupt. This bit can be protected against unprivileged access when secure with PWR SPRIV or when non-secure with PWR NSPRIV. Writing 1 to this bit clears the WUF8 flag in PWR_WUSR.

registers.name: RADIOSCR
registers.description: PWR 2.4 GHz RADIO status and control register

fields.name: MODE
fields.description: 2.4 GHz RADIO operating mode. 1x: 2.4 GHz RADIO active mode

fields.name: PHYMODE
fields.description: 2.4 GHz RADIO PHY operating mode

fields.name: ENCMODE
fields.description: 2.4 GHz RADIO encryption function operating mode

fields.name: RFVDDHPA
fields.description: 2.4 GHz RADIO VDDHPA control word. Bits [3:0] see Table 81: PA output power table format for definition. Bit [4] rf_event.

fields.name: REGPARDYVDDRFPA
fields.description: Ready bit for VsubDDHPA/sub voltage level when selecting VDDRFPA input. Note: REGPARDYVDDRFPA does not allow to detect correct VsubDDHPA/sub voltage level when request to lower the level.
